turnaround_reason: Bakkt collapsed after a -77% revenue drop and chronic GAAP losses since its 2021 SPAC. New CEO Naheta (ex-SoftBank) and a board director (Alfred) just bought $4.8M of stock at $8.20-8.34 — a rare, sizable, recent insider open-market purchase that suggests strategic confidence.
bullish_points: Director Alfred bought 585,000 shares (~$4.85M) on 2026-05-18 — material open-market buy at near-current prices
Net insider purchases of 15.8M shares over 6 months (7.5% of float), highly unusual signal
Clean balance sheet: $82M cash vs $79K debt; current ratio 4.1x
Trading below the 200-day MA but within striking distance — basing pattern with 30d +14.8%
EV/Revenue of 0.27x is dirt cheap if any revenue stabilization materializes
New CEO Naheta brings credibility and SoftBank-style scale ambition
bearish_points: Revenue down -77% YoY, two of last three earnings missed catastrophically (Q1 -475%, Q4 -1260%)
Negative gross margins (-6.9%) — business model not yet working
Single analyst coverage with target $19 lacks broad validation
Crypto-infrastructure thesis tied to volatile sentiment and regulatory winds
High short interest (20% of float) reflects continued skepticism
Operating cash flow burn ~$64M annually against $82M cash — runway is ~16 months absent fix
catalyst: Q2 earnings (Aug 10) for revenue stabilization signal; ongoing CEO strategy rollout; potential crypto-rails contracts
catalyst_timing: Q2 2026 earnings on 2026-08-10; strategic announcements in 1-3 months
suggested_trade: Spot position sized small; or Jan 2027 $10 calls for leveraged exposure
key_risk: Revenue continues to decline; CEO transition signals dilutive raise; crypto narrative reverses
would_skip_if: Q2 earnings show continued revenue cliff or operating cash burn accelerates; insider buys reverse to sells
